bullets: Par 72, 5,969 yards of Alberta golf
Six tees accommodate every handicap
18 holes in roughly 3.5 hours
Located in Athabasca County, AB
content.description_html:
Raise a glass to Athabasca Golf & Country Club, where Alberta golf meets small-town charm. Located in Athabasca County just off Highway 813, this 18-hole course offers a full day of play across six tee options, making it accessible to golfers of all skill levels.

At par 72 and just under 6,000 yards, Athabasca delivers a thoughtful routing that rewards strategy over raw power. You're looking at roughly three and a half hours to work your way around—plenty of time to enjoy the Alberta landscape and maybe grab a cold one at the turn.

This laser-etched pint glass is the perfect way to remember your round, whether you crushed it or learned some humbling lessons. Split the Tee craftsmanship meets Athabasca hospitality.
content.headline: Athabasca Golf & Country Club Pint Glass

bullets: Par 72, 6,287 yards of classic Augusta golf
18 holes with multiple tee options including Ogilvie and Harison
Expect a relaxed three-and-a-half-hour round
Located on Washington Road in Augusta, GA
content.description_html:
Raise a glass to Augusta Country Club, where 18 holes of Georgia golf await in the heart of Augusta. This classic layout plays to a par 72 across 6,287 yards of well-maintained fairways and greens. Whether you're a member or a visiting player, Augusta Country Club offers a solid test of golf with multiple tee options to suit your game.

Located on Washington Road, this course has been a fixture in the Augusta golf scene, providing golfers with a straightforward, enjoyable round. The routing gives you time to settle in—plan on about three and a half hours to play through. It's the kind of place where you can focus on your swing and enjoy the company of your foursome without pretense.

This laser-etched pint glass is the perfect way to commemorate your round or show your allegiance to one of Augusta's neighborhood gems. Split the Tee craftsmanship meets golf course pride in every sip.
content.headline: Augusta Country Club Pint Glass

bullets: 7,230 yards of uncompromising par-70 golf
148 slope rating from the White tees—bring your A-game
18 holes in approximately 3.5 hours of pure concentration
Public access to one of New York's most demanding courses
content.description_html:
Bethpage Black, nestled in Farmingdale, New York, is the kind of course that separates the confident from the cautious. At 7,230 yards with a par of 70, this beast demands respect and precision from the moment you step on the tee.

This is public golf at its most unforgiving. The course stretches across 18 holes designed to challenge every aspect of your game, with a slope rating of 148 from the White tees that'll make your handicap feel like a work of fiction. Plan for a full 3.5 hours of golf—you'll need every minute to navigate what's in front of you.

Whether you're a Long Island native or making the pilgrimage to test yourself against one of the region's most formidable layouts, Bethpage Black doesn't ask permission to humble you. It simply does.
content.headline: Bethpage Black: Long Island's Toughest Test

bullets: Par 72, 6,116 yards from the White tees
72.6 rating, 138 slope—championship caliber
18 holes of coastal California golf at its finest
Plan for a full 3.5-hour round to savor every shot
content.description_html:
Raise a glass to one of golf's most storied addresses. Pebble Beach Golf Links sits where the California coast meets championship golf, a place where the Pacific breeze and pristine fairways have hosted some of the game's greatest moments. This is the kind of course that stays with you long after your round ends.

Par 72 and playing just over 6,300 yards, Pebble Beach demands respect without apology. The White tees play to a 72.6 rating with a 138 slope—numbers that reflect the course's elegant difficulty. Eighteen holes of pure golf, where every shot matters and the scenery only makes it harder to concentrate.

Whether you've walked these fairways or dreamed of it, this laser-etched pint glass is a perfect way to remember why Pebble Beach remains one of golf's most coveted rounds. Split the Tee craftsmanship meets coastal California legend.
content.headline: Pebble Beach Golf Links Pint Glass

bullets: Par 72, 6,671 yards from the White tees
18 holes with a course rating of 73.1 and slope of 132
The legendary Road Hole (17th) awaits
Plan for roughly 3 hours 36 minutes per round
content.description_html:
The Old Course at St Andrews is golf's most storied stage. Managed by The Royal and Ancient in Fife, Scotland, it's the kind of place where every golfer wants to play at least once—and most want to play again immediately after.

This 18-hole layout plays to a par of 72 across 6,671 yards from the White tees. The course moves with the natural terrain in ways that feel both timeless and slightly mischievous. The 17th hole, the Road Hole, is the stuff of legend: a par 4 that has humbled champions and haunted their dreams for decades.

Sip from this glass and remember your round—or dream about the one you're planning. Because at St Andrews, golf isn't just a game. It's a pilgrimage.
content.headline: St Andrews Old Course Pint Glass
